maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 authorizes a local board of education to enter into a contract with any person, firm or corporation for the transportation of pupils, but it sets no maximum term, no minimum term, and no required term structure. No other provision in Article 17 of Chapter 115C (Pupil Transportation), in Chapter 143 Article 8 (Public Contracts), or in 16 NCAC 06B (State Board of Education / Student Transportation System) imposes a term limit on a pupil-transportation services contract between a local board of education and a private contractor. (Distinguish G.S. 115C-242.1(b)(2), which DOES impose a 3-year maximum with one renewal option not to exceed 3 additional years — that cap applies only to automated school bus safety camera service contracts, NOT to pupil-transportation contracts. SB 411 (2025-2026) would have extended that cap to 5 years + one 5-year renewal but, per the SOG Legislative Reporting Service, remained in the Senate Committee on Rules and Operations as of the latest action date (March 25, 2025) and was NOT enacted as of 2026-06-24.)
maxContractTerm.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 (Contracts for transportation) (last amended by Session Law 2007-423, s. 3); read in pari materia with N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-129 (Procedure for letting of public contracts) and § 143-131 (informal bidding), which by their terms reach only "construction or repair work" and "apparatus, supplies, materials, or equipment" — not services.

rebidRequired.value: Not required (by state law). North Carolina's formal-bidding statute (G.S. 143-129) and informal-bidding statute (G.S. 143-131) apply only to (1) construction or repair work and (2) purchases of apparatus, supplies, materials, or equipment — not to service contracts. The UNC School of Government, the authoritative interpreter of NC local-government contracting law, states verbatim: "There are no statutorily required procedures for contracts that do not involve purchases; construction; repair; architectural, engineering, surveying, or construction-management-at-risk services; or guaranteed energy savings. (Examples of contracts not covered by statutory procurement procedures include contracts for janitorial services, accounting services, and contracts to purchase land.)" G.S. 115C-253 itself contains no competitive-bidding requirement for pupil-transportation contracts and no requirement to rebid at the end of a term. (Federal Uniform Guidance, 2 CFR §§ 200.317–200.327, imposes its own competitive-procurement requirements where the pupil-transportation contract is paid with federal funds, but that is a federal grant-compliance overlay rather than a state-law rebidding requirement.)
rebidRequired.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-129(a) (formal bidding limited to construction/repair ≥ $500,000 or apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment ≥ $90,000; last amended by S.L. 2025-39, s. 8.5); N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-131(a) (informal bidding limited to same categories, ≥ $30,000; last amended by S.L. 2022-74, s. 40.9(b)); N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 (no bidding requirement imposed); Eileen Youens, "Local Government Purchasing and Contracting Update: Statutory Requirements and Local Policies," UNC School of Government (services contracts including janitorial, accounting, and similar are "not covered by statutory procurement procedures").
rebidRequired.sourceTier: Tier 1 — codified state statute (G.S. 143-129, 143-131, 115C-253); corroborated by Tier 4 (UNC SOG)

renewalMechanics.value: N/A by state statute. Because G.S. 115C-253 does not set a term limit and does not require competitive bidding for pupil-transportation contracts, North Carolina law neither prescribes nor restricts renewal mechanics for these contracts. Statute is silent on one-year extensions, CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, automatic rollover/evergreen renewal, mutual written-agreement renewal, agency approval, and board approval. Renewal mechanics are therefore left to the four corners of each local board's contract and to any local board policy adopted under G.S. 115C-47 and 16 NCAC 06B .0103. (Again, distinguish G.S. 115C-242.1(b)(2), which DOES address renewal — one additional term not to exceed three years — but only for automated school bus safety camera contracts.)
renewalMechanics.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 (statute silent — no renewal provision); 16 NCAC 06B .0103 (local-board policy adoption authority, readopted effective Sept. 1, 2025 — addresses safety/operational policies, not contract renewal mechanics).

statutoryCitation.value: Primary: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 ("Contracts for transportation"), Article 17, Chapter 115C of the North Carolina General Statutes (last amended by Session Law 2007-423, s. 3). Supporting (defining the scope of NC competitive-bidding law and confirming services contracts are not covered): N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-129 (formal bidding; last amended by S.L. 2025-39, s. 8.5) and § 143-131 (informal bidding; references "local school administrative unit" expressly; last amended by S.L. 2022-74, s. 40.9(b)). Administrative code: 16 NCAC 06B .0103 (local-board policies on pupil transportation; readopted Sept. 1, 2025) — imposes no term/rebid/renewal rule. Distinguishing: G.S. 115C-242.1(b)(2) (automated school bus safety camera service contracts; enacted by Session Law 2017-188, s. 2; official history credit reads "(2017-188, s. 2.)"). No NC Attorney General opinion located that interprets G.S. 115C-253 on term, bidding, or renewal.

districtVariance.value: Yes. Because state law fixes no maximum term, no rebidding requirement, and no renewal mechanics for pupil-transportation services contracts, the term length and renewal architecture of each contract is set district-by-district through local board policy and RFP terms. Publicly available examples show real variation: Wake County Public School System has structured transportation procurements with a one-year initial term plus up to four one-year renewal options at the district's sole discretion, while Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools has used a 5-year initial term with a renewal option at the Board's sole discretion. Wake County's solicitation expressly notes that 'this contract for transportation services is not required to be bid under North Carolina law' and that the district nevertheless issued an RFP to facilitate competitive selection. Variance is in contract architecture and procurement method, not in interpretation of the underlying statute (since the statute imposes no contract-architecture rule to interpret).
districtVariance.citation: Compare Wake County Public School System transportation RFP (1-year initial + up to four 1-year renewals; explicitly stating 'this contract for transportation services is not required to be bid under North Carolina law') with Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education transportation RFP (5-year initial term with renewal option at Board's sole discretion). Underlying authority: G.S. 115C-253; G.S. 143-129 (services contracts not within statutory bidding categories).
districtVariance.sourceTier: Tier 5 — district policy/RFP materials (used only to evidence variance, not to establish statewide law)

specialEducation.explanation: No North Carolina state statute or currently effective administrative regulation sets a different term limit, rebidding requirement, procurement rule, or renewal mechanic for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento (homeless) transportation, foster-care transportation, or contracts with vendors serving students with disabilities. G.S. 115C-253 applies generally to 'the transportation of pupils enrolled in the public schools of such local school administrative unit for the same purposes for which such local school administrative unit is authorized by this Article to operate public school buses' — it does not carve out special-education transportation. G.S. 115C-250 authorizes expenditures for transportation of children with disabilities but imposes no contract term, rebidding, or renewal rule. 16 NCAC 06B .0109 ('Contract Transportation of Exceptional Children') had interim-rule status conferred by S.L. 2018-114, sec. 27(b), was emergency-adopted Aug. 20, 2019, and the emergency adoption EXPIRED Dec. 6, 2019; the rule is not currently effective and was never permanently adopted, so it does not change the contracting rule today. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento impose duties to provide transportation to qualifying students (a duty-to-transport rule), but they do not impose a state-law contract-term, rebidding, or renewal rule on the LEA's contract with a private transportation contractor. Result: special-education pupil transportation is governed by the same neutral state-law framework as regular-route transportation — i.e., no statutory term limit, no statutory rebidding requirement, and no statutory renewal mechanics. (Federal Uniform Guidance procurement requirements may apply if federal funds are used, but that is a federal grant-compliance overlay, not a state-law contracting rule.)
specialEducation.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253; N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-250; 16 NCAC 06B .0109 (Contract Transportation of Exceptional Children) — emergency adoption expired Dec. 6, 2019; nothing currently effective on this point in NC administrative code.

maxContractTerm.note: Re-opened ncleg.net G.S. 115C-253 (official) — confirmed statute is silent on term limit. Re-opened all sections of Article 17 of Chapter 115C (§§ 115C-239 through 115C-259) — only § 115C-242.1(b)(2) sets a term cap, and that cap applies exclusively to automated school bus safety camera service contracts, not pupil-transportation contracts. Disconfirming search for 'term,' 'duration,' 'years,' 'not exceed,' 'contract period,' 'renewal,' 'extension' in NC statutes, administrative code, and DPI guidance produced no contrary authority. Pending SB 411 (2025-2026), which would extend the safety-camera cap to 5 years, remains in committee per UNC SOG Legislative Reporting Service and is NOT enacted as of 2026-06-24 — so even on the safety-camera side the law remains 3 + 3.

rebidRequired.note: Re-opened ncleg.net G.S. 143-129(a) and G.S. 143-131(a) (official) and the UNC SOG article (verbatim) — both statutes by their own terms reach only construction/repair work and apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment; the SOG article expressly says contracts for services (e.g., janitorial, accounting) are 'not covered by statutory procurement procedures.' G.S. 115C-253 itself imposes no bidding requirement. Disconfirming search for mandatory bidding language in school, procurement, public-contract, and district-purchasing laws produced no rule reaching transportation services contracts. WCPSS's own RFP language confirms district understanding that bidding is not required by NC law.

renewalMechanics.note: Re-opened G.S. 115C-253 — silent on renewal mechanics. Re-opened 16 NCAC 06B Subchapter rules — only .0103 addresses local-board policy adoption and is limited to safety/operational policies (bus use, driver recruitment, passenger safety, monitor duties); no renewal provision. Disconfirming search for CPI caps, fuel-adjustment statutes, board/state-approval requirements, annual-appropriations requirements, or maximum-renewal-count statutes governing pupil transportation services produced no contrary authority.

statutoryCitation.note: All primary citations (G.S. 115C-253; G.S. 143-129; G.S. 143-131; 16 NCAC 06B .0103) re-opened and confirmed verbatim. Minor correction to Stage 1: the Stage 1 pack stated G.S. 115C-242.1 was enacted by 'Session Law 2017-188, s. 1.' The official ncleg.gov page for G.S. 115C-242.1 displays the history credit as '(2017-188, s. 2.)' — the section number is s. 2, not s. 1. Also, Stage 1 referenced 2018-114 as amending § 115C-242.1; that reference is not visible in the current statutory history credit. S.L. 2018-114, sec. 27(b) instead appears as 'interim rule status' authority in the history note of 16 NCAC 06B .0109 (the exceptional children admin-code rule that expired Dec. 6, 2019). These are bookkeeping corrections, not substantive — the 3-year + one 3-year-renewal rule of § 115C-242.1(b)(2) is verified and that statute remains a distinguishing source, not authority over pupil-transportation contracts.

districtVariance.note: Confirmed and strengthened: in addition to Wake County's 1-year + four 1-year renewals architecture (with explicit recital that NC law does not require bidding), Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools uses a 5-year initial term with a renewal option at Board sole discretion — two large LEAs, two different architectures, both consistent with statutory silence.

specialEducation.note: Re-opened 16 NCAC 06B .0109 history note via OAH publication — confirmed 'Interim Rule status conferred Eff. June 27, 2018, pursuant to S.L. 2018-114, sec. 27.(b); Emergency Adoption Eff. August 20, 2019; Emergency Adoption Expired Eff. December 6, 2019.' Rule is not currently effective. No other NC statute or currently effective administrative rule imposes a different term/rebid/renewal rule on special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or disability transportation contracts. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento create duty-to-transport obligations, not state-law contracting rules.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term — no statutory maximum
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An overlooked NC statute or administrative rule (in Article 17 of Chapter 115C, Chapter 143 Article 8, or 16 NCAC 06B) imposes a max term on pupil-transportation services contracts; OR § 115C-242.1's cap actually reaches such contracts; OR a 2025 session law amended § 115C-253 or § 115C-242.1 to add a cap reaching pupil-transportation services.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened ncleg.net/ncleg.gov index of Article 17 sections (§§ 239–259); read § 115C-242.1 verbatim; ran web searches for '2025 NC session law school bus pupil transportation contract amendment'; reviewed SOG Legislative Reporting Service for SB 411 (2025-2026); searched NC bill tracker for any post-2007 amendment to § 115C-253.
challengeMatrix.result: Disconfirming hypothesis rejected. § 115C-253 has not been amended since S.L. 2007-423, s. 3. No other section in Article 17 imposes a term cap on pupil-transportation contracts. § 115C-242.1(b)(2)'s 3-year cap (history credit '(2017-188, s. 2.)') reaches only automated school bus safety camera service contracts. SB 411 (2025) would have extended that cap to 5 + 5 but remains in committee and is NOT enacted.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed; confidence preserved at High.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required — Not required by state law
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A NC statute outside G.S. 143-129/143-131 imposes mandatory competitive bidding on service contracts (including pupil-transportation services); OR § 115C-253 implicitly reaches a competitive-procurement requirement; OR an Attorney General opinion or DPI guidance memo construes the framework as requiring bidding.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read G.S. 143-129(a) and 143-131(a) verbatim — both expressly limited to construction/repair and apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment. Re-opened UNC SOG (Youens) article verbatim — confirms 'no statutorily required procedures' for non-purchase, non-construction, non-A/E/S/CMAR, non-energy-savings contracts; janitorial and accounting service contracts cited as examples not covered. Searched for any NC AG opinion interpreting § 115C-253 — none located. Searched NC DPI fiscal guidance — references the $90,000 threshold of 143-129 but does not extend it to services.
challengeMatrix.result: Disconfirming hypothesis rejected. The statutes do not reach service contracts and the SOG (authoritative interpreter) confirms.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed; confidence preserved at High.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics — N/A by state statute
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: 16 NCAC 06B or another administrative rule imposes a renewal-mechanic rule (CPI cap, fuel adjustment, board-approval requirement, annual-appropriations limit, max-renewals limit) on pupil-transportation services contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read 16 NCAC 06B Subchapter rules PDF (OAH official) — only .0103, .0107, .0111, .0112 are currently effective and substantive; none address renewal mechanics. Searched for any 'renewal,' 'extension,' 'option,' or 'evergreen' provision in NC school-transportation regulations — none.
challengeMatrix.result: Disconfirming hypothesis rejected. Statute and admin code are silent; renewal is governed by contract and local policy.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed; confidence preserved at High.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation — primary is § 115C-253; supporting are §§ 143-129, 143-131; admin code is 16 NCAC 06B .0103
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: The cited sources misstate or contain wrong session-law section numbers; the cited admin code is not currently effective; a more directly controlling section exists.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-fetched each primary URL from ncleg.net/ncleg.gov and the OAH subchapter PDF. Verified each statute's current text and session-law history line. Cross-checked § 115C-242.1's history credit.
challengeMatrix.result: Substantive citations all verified verbatim. Two minor bookkeeping corrections to Stage 1 (now reflected in the reconciled pack and noted in verdicts.statutoryCitation): (i) § 115C-242.1 history credit is '(2017-188, s. 2.)', not s. 1; (ii) the reference to 2018-114 as an amendment of § 115C-242.1 is not visible in the official history line — S.L. 2018-114, sec. 27(b) appears instead as the conferring authority for interim-rule status of 16 NCAC 06B .0109.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Substantive citations confirmed; minor citation-text errors corrected. No change to substance. Confidence preserved at High.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: All NC LEAs use the same contract architecture, indicating uniform statewide practice rather than variance.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Compared at least two LEAs: (i) Wake County Public School System transportation RFP (1-year initial + up to four 1-year renewals; explicit recital that NC law does not require bidding); (ii) Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education transportation RFP (5-year initial term with renewal option at Board's sole discretion).
challengeMatrix.result: Disconfirming hypothesis rejected. Two large LEAs use materially different term/renewal architectures. Variance evidenced.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed and strengthened with a second district example.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate NC statute or currently effective administrative rule imposes different contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules for special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student, alternative, or disability transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read 16 NCAC 06B .0109 history note — emergency adoption expired Dec. 6, 2019; not currently effective. Searched Article 9 of Chapter 115C (Education of Children with Disabilities) and § 115C-250 — neither imposes a contract architecture rule. Searched NC DPI Exceptional Children policies — duty-to-transport rule under IDEA, no state contracting rule.
challengeMatrix.result: Disconfirming hypothesis rejected. No NC state law sets separate contracting rules for special-ed transportation; the .0109 rule that would have addressed exceptional-children contract transportation lapsed before permanent adoption.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed; confidence preserved at High.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: § 115C-253 or § 115C-242.1 was amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the code-currency dates Stage 1 recorded; or a 2025-2026 session law enacted a new contract-term/rebid/renewal rule for pupil transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Checked SOG Legislative Reporting Service bill page for SB 411; checked S.L. 2025-47 (the only 2025 transportation-camera session law) — SL 2025-47 (SB 391) addresses school-zone speed cameras and does not amend § 115C-253 or § 115C-242.1. Confirmed SB 411 remains in Senate Committee on Rules and Operations and is NOT enacted.
challengeMatrix.result: No post-2007 amendment to § 115C-253. No 2025/2026 amendment to § 115C-242.1.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Finding confirmed; confidence preserved at High.

sourcesReverified.note: Re-opened on ncleg.net (official). Confirmed statute authorizes contracting in lieu of district operation; imposes no term, no bidding, no renewal rule. History credits include S.L. 2007-423, s. 3 as last amendment.

Re-opened on ncleg.net (official). Confirmed (a) reaches only construction/repair >= $500,000 and apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment >= $90,000; (e) exceptions all relate to apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment categories. Last amended by S.L. 2025-39, s. 8.5.

Re-opened on ncleg.net (official). Confirmed informal-bid range ($30,000 to G.S. 143-129 thresholds) is limited to same categories; the statute expressly references 'local school administrative unit.' Last amended by S.L. 2022-74, s. 40.9(b).

Re-opened OAH subchapter PDF via firecrawl_scrape. Confirmed .0103 readopted Eff. Sept. 1, 2025 (local-board policy adoption, no contract-term/rebid/renewal rule); .0109 emergency adoption expired Dec. 6, 2019 (not currently effective); .0111 effective Oct. 1, 2020 (passenger safety); .0112 effective Dec. 1, 2020 (purchase of buses from statewide term contracts under G.S. 143-49). None impose a contract-architecture rule on pupil-transportation services contracts.

Re-opened on ncleg.gov (official). Confirmed subdivision (b)(2): 'The maximum length of any contract entered into under this subdivision shall be three years. A contract entered into under this subdivision may contain an option to renew or extend the contract for only one additional term not to exceed three years.' Confirmed verbatim history credit '(2017-188, s. 2.)' — Stage 1's reference to 'Session Law 2017-188, s. 1' and to amendment by '2018-114' is corrected: the official credit reads s. 2 only. S.L. 2018-114, sec. 27(b) appears in 16 NCAC 06B .0109's history note (interim rule status), not in § 115C-242.1's history.

sourcesReverified.citation: Eileen Youens, 'Local Government Purchasing and Contracting Update: Statutory Requirements and Local Policies,' UNC School of Government
sourcesReverified.note: Re-opened via firecrawl_scrape with PDF parser. Verbatim quote confirmed: 'There are no statutorily required procedures for contracts that do not involve purchases; construction; repair; architectural, engineering, surveying, or construction-management-at-risk services; or guaranteed energy savings. (Examples of contracts not covered by statutory procurement procedures include contracts for janitorial services, accounting services, and contracts to purchase land.)'

sourcesReverified.citation: NC DPI School Transportation laws & policies hub (ncbussafety.org)
sourcesReverified.note: Re-opened. Hub cross-links to Article 17 of Chapter 115C, 16 NCAC 06B, and NC State Board policies. No source identified in the hub imposes a state-law contract-term, rebidding, or renewal rule on pupil-transportation services contracts.

sourcesReverified.citation: Wake County Public School System transportation RFP
sourcesReverified.note: Re-confirmed via WebSearch: WCPSS structures transportation procurement as a 1-year initial term with up to four 1-year renewal options at district sole discretion, and the solicitation expressly recites that 'this contract for transportation services is not required to be bid under North Carolina law' but that WCPSS uses an RFP to facilitate competition.

sourcesReverified.citation: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education transportation RFP (added — district-variance corroboration)
sourcesReverified.note: Re-confirmed via WebSearch: CMS uses a 5-year initial term with renewal option at the Board's sole discretion. Second-district datapoint corroborates district variance (added in Stage 2; not present in Stage 1).

sourcesReverified.citation: SB 411 (2025-2026) status (school bus cameras / evidence and contract length)
sourcesReverified.note: Re-confirmed via UNC SOG Legislative Reporting Service: SB 411 was filed March 24, 2025, passed first reading March 25, 2025, and was referred to Senate Committee on Rules and Operations the same day. Remains pending; NOT enacted as of 2026-06-24. Therefore § 115C-242.1's 3-year + one 3-year-renewal cap remains the current law (and remains limited to automated school bus safety camera service contracts).

S.L. 2025-47 addresses school-zone speed cameras (effective Oct. 1, 2025). Does not amend § 115C-253 or § 115C-242.1; does not impose any term/rebid/renewal rule on pupil-transportation services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-253 ('Contracts for transportation'), Article 17, Chapter 115C
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Last amended by Session Law 2007-423, s. 3 (no later amendment located through 2025-2026 session). Statute as displayed on the official North Carolina General Assembly site.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special Education
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Any local board of education may, in lieu of the operation by it of public school buses, enter into a contract with any person, firm or corporation for the transportation by such person, firm or corporation of pupils enrolled in the public schools of such local school administrative unit for the same purposes for which such local school administrative unit is authorized by this Article to operate public school buses.' The section addresses authority, vehicle/driver qualification, and funding only — no term limit, no bidding requirement, no renewal mechanism.

sourceLedger.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-129 ('Procedure for letting of public contracts')
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Last amended by Session Law 2025-39, s. 8.5.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section (a) reaches only 'construction or repair work requiring the estimated expenditure of public money in an amount equal to or more than five hundred thousand dollars ($500,000) or purchase of apparatus, supplies, materials, or equipment requiring an estimated expenditure of public money in an amount equal to or more than ninety thousand dollars ($90,000).' Subsection (e) exceptions all relate to the same apparatus/supplies/materials/equipment categories. The statute does not reach service contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 143-131 ('When counties, cities, towns and other subdivisions may let contracts on informal bids')
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Last amended by Session Law 2022-74, s. 40.9(b).
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'All contracts for construction or repair work or for the purchase of apparatus, supplies, materials, or equipment, involving the expenditure of public money in the amount of thirty thousand dollars ($30,000) or more, but less than the limits prescribed in G.S. 143-129, made by any...local school administrative unit...shall be made after informal bids have been secured.' By its own terms it does not reach service contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: 16 NCAC 06B (Student Transportation System), including .0103 (Local Policies, Rules, and Regulations for Public School Transportation Programs); .0109 (Contract Transportation of Exceptional Children); .0111 (School Bus Passengers); .0112 (Purchase of School Buses and Equipment)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: OAH subchapter PDF. .0103 readopted Eff. September 1, 2025; .0107 readopted Eff. September 1, 2025; .0111 Eff. October 1, 2020; .0112 Eff. December 1, 2020; .0109 (Contract Transportation of Exceptional Children) emergency adoption expired December 6, 2019 (not currently effective).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special Education differentiation (none currently effective); confirms absence of any administrative-code term/renewal/bid rule on pupil-transportation contracts.
sourceLedger.relevantText: .0103 requires each local board to adopt policies on use of buses, driver recruitment, passenger safety, etc., but imposes no contract-term, rebidding, or renewal rule. .0109 (Exceptional Children) emergency adoption expired Dec. 6, 2019 — not currently in force. .0112 requires LEAs to purchase BUSES through statewide term contracts under G.S. 143-49, but says nothing about contracting with a private operator for transportation SERVICES.

sourceLedger.citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 115C-242.1 ('Automated school bus safety cameras'), subsection (b)(2)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: History credit reads '(2017-188, s. 2.)' — enacted by Session Law 2017-188, s. 2. No subsequent amendment visible in the current statute's history line. SB 411 (2025-2026), which would extend the 3-year cap to 5 years, remains in Senate committee and is NOT enacted.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: (Distinguishing source) confirms that when the NC legislature wants to set a contract term cap for school-bus-related service contracts, it does so explicitly — here, 3-year maximum with one renewal not to exceed 3 additional years. The absence of equivalent language in § 115C-253 is meaningful.
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The maximum length of any contract entered into under this subdivision shall be three years. A contract entered into under this subdivision may contain an option to renew or extend the contract for only one additional term not to exceed three years.' (Applies to automated safety-camera service contracts, not to pupil-transportation services contracts.)

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — authoritative interpretive guidance (UNC School of Government)
sourceLedger.citation: Eileen Youens, 'Local Government Purchasing and Contracting Update: Statutory Requirements and Local Policies,' UNC School of Government
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Article addresses the statutory framework of G.S. Ch. 143 Art. 8 as applied to local governments and school administrative units (current at time of publication; the cited bidding thresholds match those in the post-2022 amended statutes).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; confirms scope of NC competitive-bidding law
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'There are no statutorily required procedures for contracts that do not involve purchases; construction; repair; architectural, engineering, surveying, or construction-management-at-risk services; or guaranteed energy savings. (Examples of contracts not covered by statutory procurement procedures include contracts for janitorial services, accounting services, and contracts to purchase land.)' — i.e., pupil-transportation SERVICES contracts are not within the statutory bidding categories.

sourceLedger.citation: North Carolina Department of Public Instruction — School Transportation laws & policies hub (ncbussafety.org)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Portal cross-links to the official Article 17 of Chapter 115C, 16 NCAC 06B, and NC State Board policies.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms the universe of NC pupil-transportation authority — no source on the hub imposes a state-law contract term/rebid/renewal rule.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Lists Article 17 of Chapter 115C, 16 NCAC 06B, NC State Board of Education policies, and ancillary statutes (Umstead Act, tort claims, motor-vehicle laws) as the controlling framework; no statute or rule in the list governs contract term, rebidding, or renewal of pupil-transportation services contracts.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 5 — district practice (used to evidence variance only, not state law)
sourceLedger.citation: Wake County Public School System transportation RFP (term and bidding-applicability language)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Initial term of one year with up to four one-year renewal options at the district's sole discretion; explicit recital that 'this contract for transportation services is not required to be bid under North Carolina law' — evidence that LEAs themselves understand G.S. 143-129 not to reach pupil-transportation services and that contract architecture is district-defined.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 5 — district practice (added in Stage 2 to corroborate variance)
sourceLedger.citation: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education transportation RFP
sourceLedger.relevantText: Initial term of five years with a renewal option at the Board's sole discretion at end of the initial term — materially different architecture from Wake County's 1+4, evidencing genuine variance across NC LEAs.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — UNC SOG Legislative Reporting Service
sourceLedger.citation: SB 411 (2025-2026) status page — 'School Bus Cameras / Evidence & Contract Length'
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Bill referred to Senate Committee on Rules and Operations March 25, 2025; remains pending as of 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Currentness check on § 115C-242.1; confirms no 2025/2026 amendment to that section.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Bill would extend § 115C-242.1 contract cap from 3 + 3 years to 5 + 5 years; not enacted; current cap remains 3 + 3.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Per-state research limited to North Carolina. Approach: (1) read each cited statute and admin-code section verbatim on the official ncleg.net / ncleg.gov and OAH publication sites; (2) re-fetch the UNC SOG article via firecrawl_scrape with PDF parser to capture the verbatim services-not-covered language; (3) cross-check Article 17 of Chapter 115C section-by-section for any provision imposing a contract-term, rebidding, or renewal rule on pupil-transportation services contracts; (4) cross-check Chapter 143 Article 8 for any general statute reaching services; (5) cross-check 16 NCAC 06B subchapter rules; (6) cross-check 2025-2026 session laws and the SOG Legislative Reporting Service for amendments; (7) cross-check at least two LEA RFPs to evidence district variance.

verificationNotes.codeCurrency: G.S. 115C-253: last amended by Session Law 2007-423, s. 3 (no later amendment located). G.S. 143-129: last amended by S.L. 2025-39, s. 8.5. G.S. 143-131: last amended by S.L. 2022-74, s. 40.9(b). G.S. 115C-242.1: history credit reads '(2017-188, s. 2.)' (Stage 1's reference to 's. 1' is corrected). Statutes read on the official North Carolina General Assembly website.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: 16 NCAC 06B (Student Transportation System) reviewed via NC Office of Administrative Hearings publication; key rules — .0103 (local-board policies, readopted Sept. 1, 2025); .0107 (DPI evaluation, readopted Sept. 1, 2025); .0111 (passenger safety, effective Oct. 1, 2020); .0112 (purchase of buses from statewide term contracts, effective Dec. 1, 2020); .0109 (Contract Transportation of Exceptional Children) was emergency-adopted Aug. 20, 2019 and the emergency adoption expired Dec. 6, 2019 — the rule is not currently effective.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Checked NC General Assembly bill tracker, SOG Legislative Reporting Service, and 2025 session laws for any post-2007 amendment to G.S. 115C-253 — none located. The most recent amendment to G.S. 115C-253 remains S.L. 2007-423, s. 3. The 2025 session-law activity touching school-bus contracts (S 411) addresses § 115C-242.1 (automated school bus safety cameras), would extend the cap from 3+3 to 5+5, and remains pending in Senate committee (not enacted) as of 2026-06-24. S.L. 2025-47 (SB 391) addresses school-zone speed cameras (not school-bus cameras and not pupil-transportation contracts). G.S. 143-129 was most recently amended in 2025 (S.L. 2025-39, s. 8.5) but the amendment did not extend the statute to reach service contracts.
negativeSearchPaths: Searched ncleg.net section of G.S. 115C-253 for any term/renewal/competitive-bidding language — none present.

Searched ncleg.net Article 17 of Chapter 115C (G.S. 115C-239 through 115C-259) for any term limit, rebidding requirement, or renewal mechanic applicable to private pupil-transportation contracts — none located. The only term cap in Chapter 115C is G.S. 115C-242.1(b)(2) for automated school bus safety camera service contracts.

Searched G.S. 143-129 and 143-131 for any language extending bidding requirements to service contracts — the statutes by their own terms reach only 'construction or repair work' and 'apparatus, supplies, materials, or equipment.' No 'services' category exists.

Searched 16 NCAC 06B (Student Transportation System) for any administrative-code term/renewal/bid rule on private pupil-transportation contracts — none located. .0109 (would have addressed exceptional-children contract transportation) lapsed Dec. 6, 2019 and was not permanently adopted.

Searched the NC Department of Justice opinions directory and ran general queries for any NC AG opinion interpreting G.S. 115C-253 on term length, rebidding, or renewal — no responsive opinion located.
Searched NC DPI School Transportation page and ncbussafety.org for DPI/State Board guidance imposing a contract-term, rebid, or renewal rule on private pupil-transportation contracts — none located.

Searched 2025-2026 NC General Assembly session laws and bill tracker for amendments to G.S. 115C-253 post-2007-423; none located. The 2025 session-law activity touching school-bus contracts (S 411) addresses automated school bus safety cameras under G.S. 115C-242.1 — not pupil-transportation services contracts — and is not enacted.
Searched for Article 9 of Chapter 115C (Education of Children with Disabilities) provisions imposing a different contract-term/rebid/renewal rule for special-education transportation — none located.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict between authoritative sources surfaced. Two minor bookkeeping corrections from Stage 1 to Stage 2: (i) § 115C-242.1 history credit is '(2017-188, s. 2.)', not s. 1; (ii) Stage 1's reference to amendment of § 115C-242.1 by S.L. 2018-114 was not corroborated by the official statute history — S.L. 2018-114 sec. 27(b) instead appears as the interim-rule-status authority for 16 NCAC 06B .0109. Neither correction affects the substantive findings on the five fields.
